note: Official expectation model only - live integration NOT executed and NO compatibility/certification is claimed.
authMethod: HTTP Basic auth (username:password) over TLS, or OAuth2 bearer token via /oauth_token.do. Basic is common for service accounts; OAuth2 preferred for delegated/rotating credentials.

expectedRequestShape.create: POST /api/now/table/incident  header Accept: application/json, Content-Type: application/json  body { "short_description": "...", "caller_id": "..." }
expectedRequestShape.requirement: Account has the table-level ACLs and roles (e.g. itil) for the operation; respect sysparm_limit for paging.

expectedFailureResponseShape.http: 401 invalid credentials  OR  403 ACL/role denied (User Not Authorized)  OR  400 invalid field/query  OR  404 record/table not found

commonIntegrationFailure: 403 'ACL Exception / User Not Authorized' when the integration user lacks the table role; 400 when sysparm_query references a non-existent field or bad encoded query; silent truncation when sysparm_limit/offset paging is ignored.
diagnosticCommand: GET /api/now/table/sys_user?sysparm_limit=1 to confirm auth+role; validate sysparm_query field names against the table dictionary; check X-Total-Count header for paging.
preventionRule: Grant the integration user the minimum table role; always page with sysparm_limit + sysparm_offset and honor X-Total-Count; validate encoded queries against the table schema; prefer OAuth2 with rotating secrets over static Basic credentials.
